preview_text:
The model favours Senegal at 60% to win, with Iraq given a 13% chance of victory. The draw probability sits at 27%, suggesting meaningful likelihood of a stalemate on a neutral pitch.

The win-loss gap reflects a moderate favourite in Senegal, but the 27% draw share indicates the match is not expected to be a runaway. Iraq's double-digit win probability signals competitive balance rather than a mismatch; the outcome remains genuinely open across all three results.

The market consensus across sportsbooks leans more decisively toward Senegal at 66%, a gap of roughly 6 percentage points above the model. Polymarket prices align more closely with the sportsbook view, pushing Senegal higher still at 69%. The model and books agree closely on Iraq's floor near 12–13%, but diverge on whether Senegal's edge is as wide as the market suggests. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these views.
